questionsJunior.text:
Welcome, __NAME__!
 Ready for a fun adventure?
 Complete 12 fun challenges and find magic words hidden around the festival!
What colour do you get when you mix red and blue?
How many legs does a spider have?
I have hands but I can't clap. What am I?
Find the Guardian of the Flame and ask for his magic item.
What is a baby dog called?
Visit the main stage and look for the secret symbol.
Go to the Flipbook Station for your next clue.
I'm tall when I'm young and short when I'm old. What am I?
Find one of our Children's Coordinators and ask for the magic word.

Amazing work, __NAME__!
 You finished the Junior Scavenger Hunt!
 You're a superstar! 🌟

questionsSenior.text:
Welcome, __NAME__.
 This is meant to be QUITE DIFFICULT.
 21 brain-bending challenges.
 The top 5 scores win actual Bitcoin
 - the hardest money ever created.
 Think you're up for it?

Your first challenge is to identify the next letter in this sequence:
M,V,E,M,J,?
questionsSenior.subtext: Hint: Think outside the box, in fact think WAY outside the box.

questionsSenior.text: Your next challenge is to find the Guardian of the Flame and identify his magic item.

questionsSenior.subtext: Hint: This is Morse code. Dots are short signals, dashes are long signals, and spaces separate letters.

questionsSenior.text:
Look for the quadrilateral puzzle somewhere on the festival grounds
A map might be helpful for this one.
questionsSenior.subtext: Hint: Don't forget to count the quadrilaterals of all dimensions. including the clue itself!

questionsSenior.text:
Play the Reunion DevOps guy in a 5-minute chess game (5|0) on the physical board and clock.
If you win, boastfully present your your phone to collect your reward.
If you lose, wait 1 hour and try again.
If you can't win don't worry ... it's only 1 point.

What 5-digit number has the following properties?
- All digits are different
- The digit in the ten-thousands place is three times the digit in the tens place
- The digit in the thousands place is one less than the digit in the tens place
- The digit in the ones place is two more than the digit in the thousands place
- The digit in the hundreds place is four more than the digit in the thousands place
- The full number is divisible by 7

What is the next number in this sequence:
1,4,1,5,9,2,6?
questionsSenior.subtext: Hint: This sequence is infinite and non-repeating. The ratio of circle's circumference to its diameter is an important mathematical constant that starts with these digits.

questionsSenior.text:
Great! Now, solve this binary puzzle:
The decimal equivalent of the binary number 1100110 is 102 as shown here.
 What is the decimal equivalent of the binary number 01000101?
What is the only element that is a liquid metal at room temperature?
questionsSenior.subtext: Hint: It shares its name with a planet.

questionsSenior.text: What block height was Laurel Cardiff-Das born?
questionsSenior.subtext: Hint: Our Vectors & Logos expert might know the answer to this one.

questionsSenior.text:
What are the missing words?
52 c_ in a d_ without j_
questionsSenior.subtext: Hint: You must be royally flushed to get this one.

questionsSenior.text:
White to move and checkmate in two moves.
 Only the first move is required for the answer.
Use standard chess notation.
questionsSenior.subtext:
Hint: Look for a move that puts black in check, even if it seems to lose material at first.
Are you missing a "+" for check?
The eventual plan is Rd8#

questionsSenior.text:
What is the next row of numbers in this sequence?
1
1,1
2,1
1,2,1,1,
1,1,1,2,1,1
3,1,1,2,1,1
questionsSenior.subtext: Hint: The rows are self referential. Each row describes the previous row. The first row is "one 1" -> 1,1. The second row is "two 1s" -> 2,1. The third row is "one 2, then one 1" -> 1,2,1,1, etc.

questionsSenior.text: Find one of our Children's Coordinators and ask for the magic word.

What are the missing words?
11 p_ on a s_ t_
What is the hometown of last year's international headliner?
What something the maker doesn't need, the buyer doesn't use, and the user doesn't see?
questionsSenior.subtext:
Hint: This is a classic riddle that has been around for centuries.
It describes something that is associated with death, but it is not alive itself.

questionsSenior.text:
Decode this encrypted message to find the magic word:
 fhcrepnyvsentvyvfgvprkcvnyvqbpvbhf
questionsSenior.subtext:
Hint: This is a ROT13 cypher - type the letter "a" and see what letter it becomes.
What doyou notice about the relationship between the original letters and the transformed letters?

questionsSenior.text:
What are the missing words?
88 k_ on a g_ p_.
Complete the Flipbook Station for your next clue.

Find a number that has a SHA256 output with 2 leading zeros
(Example Hash: 00abcdef...)
Use trial and error.
P.S. this is very similar to how Bitcoin mining works!

Well done, __NAME__.
 You've completed the Senior Scavenger Hunt.
 If your score is in the top 5, you're in the running for Bitcoin.
 Submit your score to enter.
